name: Stardog Enterprise Knowledge Graph API
what_it_does: Enterprise knowledge graph platform with HTTP REST API — enabling organizations to build and query knowledge graphs via SPARQL, connect heterogeneous data sources (databases, files, APIs) into a unified graph, apply OWL reasoning for intelligent inference, and use Stardog Studio for visual exploration, with a focus on enterprise AI and data fabric use cases.
use_cases: Building knowledge graphs via Stardog API for connecting disparate enterprise data sources
Querying linked data via Stardog SPARQL endpoint for complex relationship traversal
Applying OWL reasoning via Stardog API for intelligent data inference and classification
Integrating virtual graphs via Stardog API for query federation across databases without ETL
Building AI-ready knowledge graphs via Stardog for LLM grounding and semantic search
not_for: Simple property graph queries (use Neo4j for Cypher-based property graphs)
High-throughput operational databases (Stardog is analytical, not OLTP)
Teams without RDF/SPARQL expertise or willingness to learn knowledge graph concepts
best_when: An enterprise data team needs to integrate and reason over complex, heterogeneous data sources using standards-based RDF/SPARQL/OWL — especially for data fabric, compliance, or AI grounding use cases.
avoid_when: You need property graph (Cypher), high-throughput OLTP, or lack semantic web / knowledge graph expertise.

auth.notes: HTTP Basic auth (username:password). API token: Authorization: Bearer {token} (JWT from /admin/token). Admin REST API: /admin/ endpoints for database management. SPARQL endpoint: /db/{database}/query for SPARQL queries. Virtual graphs: /admin/virtual_graphs for data federation config. Kerberos/LDAP for enterprise SSO. Role-based access control (RBAC) via Stardog roles. Self-hosted only; Stardog Cloud: managed offering. CLI: stardog-admin for management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Community edition: free; limited to 1 user, 1 database
pricing.notes: US company (Washington, DC). Founded 2012 by Kendall Clark, Evren Sirin, and Mike Grove (ex-University of Maryland). Knowledge graph and semantic web experts. Stardog Studio: visual UI for graph exploration. Stardog Cloud: managed hosted Stardog. Stardog Data Fabric: enterprise data integration product. Virtual graphs: query relational databases (SQL), NoSQL, APIs as if they were RDF — no ETL. Reasoning: OWL 2 RL and EL profiles. SPARQL: SPARQL 1.1 compliant + Stardog extensions (property paths, geospatial). Stored queries: named queries for reuse. Machine learning integration: SPARQL ML for in-graph model execution.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; depends on cluster size

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Basic auth or Bearer JWT. SPARQL endpoint at /db/{db}/query. Admin API at /admin/. Virtual graphs for data federation. OWL reasoning. SPARQL 1.1 required knowledge. Community edition (free). Self-hosted primarily.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: SPARQL SELECT/CONSTRUCT/ASK queries idempotent. SPARQL UPDATE: not idempotent (use transactions). Transactions: Stardog supports multi-statement SPARQL transactions. Virtual graph queries: idempotent for read-only sources.
known_agent_gotchas: SPARQL knowledge required: Stardog uses SPARQL 1.1 (not Cypher/Gremlin) — steep learning curve for non-RDF developers
Admin API (/admin/) vs data API (/db/{db}/) are separate with different auth contexts
JWT token obtained via POST /admin/token with Basic auth — use for subsequent requests
Virtual graphs: query federation requires JDBC drivers for SQL databases — setup complexity
OWL reasoning must be configured per-database (schema layer) before inference queries work
SPARQL UPDATE statements are NOT idempotent — wrap in transactions for safe retries

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Bearer JWT from admin token endpoint. RBAC with Stardog roles — fine-grained database access control. SOC2 certified. Knowledge graph data may contain sensitive enterprise IP — restrict access by role. Kerberos/LDAP for enterprise IdP integration. Self-hosted: operator responsible for security. Virtual graphs: federated queries access source databases — source credentials must be secured in Stardog config. Admin API: high-privilege — network-restrict to admin hosts.
